news.title: HDFC Property Fund, HDFC Capital Advisors pay ₹26 lakh to settle regulatory violations
news.para: HDFC Property Fund and HDFC Capital Advisors have settled a Sebi case for Rs 26 lakh concerning delayed liquidation and distribution of assets for two schemes. The funds, HIREF and HITCF, faced significant delays, with HIREF taking about seven years to wind up. HDFC Capital Advisors, the current investment manager, ensured the final distribution of retained amounts to investors.

news.title: India needs to accelerate construction pace to meet urbanisation demand: Housing minister
news.para: Housing and Urban Affairs Minister Manohar Lal Khattar said every building should carry a durability certificate indicating its expected lifespan. Speaking at Bharat Buildcon 2026, he also called for faster adoption of prefabricated construction, 3D printing, engineered bamboo and recycled construction materials.

news.title: Knowledge Realty Trust to raise up to ₹2,000 crore via NCDs, commercial papers
news.para: The borrowing committee of Knowledge Realty Office Management Services, manager to Knowledge Realty Trust, has approved raising up to ₹1,000 crore through non-convertible debentures and up to ₹1,000 crore through commercial papers.

news.title: Patna civic body's property tax collection crosses ₹31 crore
news.para: Patna Municipal Corporation has collected over Rs 31 crore in property tax this financial year, exceeding last year's collection despite fewer taxpayers. This increase is attributed to a revised assessment structure and improved compliance, with online payments remaining dominant.

news.title: Bihar government relaxes rules for selling land in proposed township areas
news.para: Bihar's cabinet has approved land acquisition for new satellite townships. Restrictions on land sales in these areas are now relaxed for landowners facing crises. The cabinet also sanctioned funds for the Varanasi-Ranchi-Kolkata Expressway and an Intelligent Traffic Management System. Plans are underway to develop tourism destinations and enhance facilities for athletes.

news.title: Bhubaneswar development body announces four new town planning schemes covering 969 acres
news.para: The Bhubaneswar Development Authority (BDA) has unveiled four new town planning schemes, Samrudha Sahar, encompassing 969 acres to foster planned urbanisation and economic growth. This initiative aims to transform the city into dynamic growth centers by developing essential infrastructure and services.

news.title: Pune: PMRDA told to bring structural plan in 12-18 months to unlock stalled permissions
news.para: Pune's Urban Development Minister Madhuri Misal has ordered the Pune Metropolitan Region Development Authority (PMRDA) to expedite its structural plan. This plan is crucial for resolving delays in development permissions. The minister also pushed for progress on town planning schemes, the ring road, and a twin-tunnel project. Citizens can expect interim building permission relief.

news.title: Mumbai: Developers, industries consider tankers, recycling options amid water supply curbs
news.para: Mumbai's water supply cuts are prompting developers and industries to invest in water recycling and reuse. Facing higher costs and potential project delays due to a 20% cut on industrial and commercial establishments and a suspension for construction sites, businesses are accelerating adoption of sewage treatment plants and rainwater harvesting.

news.title: JK Cement's plant project faces pushback in Bathinda village
news.para: Activists and locals in Bathinda's Maiserkhana village vehemently opposed a proposed JK Cement plant during a public hearing, citing environmental concerns and misclassification of the industry. Protesters demanded the hearing be voided due to the company's failure to disclose a full Environmental Impact Assessment report and address water usage concerns.

news.title: Karnataka high court to government: Formulate full-scale homestay policy
news.para: The Karnataka High Court has urged the tourism department to develop a comprehensive policy for homestays, following an incident of alleged assault in Kodagu. The court emphasized the need for clear regulations covering tourist safety, food and fire safety, building compliance, and guest security to enhance confidence in the state as a destination.

news.title: Chandigarh master plan amendments to unlock housing potential, ease affordability
news.para: Chandigarh is set to expand housing and mixed-use development with proposed Master Plan amendments. The city aims to unlock vacant land, permit higher-density construction, and increase affordability. Key changes include nearly doubling mixed land use areas and encouraging vertical growth, addressing the growing housing demand and making home ownership more accessible to residents.

news.title: Delhi slum and JJ Cluster rehabilitation and resettlement policy finalised: CM
news.para: Delhi's Chief Minister Rekha Gupta announced the finalization of the Slum and JJ Cluster Rehabilitation and Resettlement Policy, 2026. The first phase will see five JJ clusters rehabilitated with basic amenities, with tendering to begin within 45 days. This initiative aims to provide dignified housing for slum residents across the city.

news.title: ACC to pursue calibrated expansion of cement capacity amid strong infra demand: Chairman
news.para: ACC Ltd plans calibrated capacity expansion, deeper RMC footprint, and accelerated adoption of low-carbon technologies, according to Chairman Karan Adani.  The company aims to evolve into integrated building materials solution providers, leveraging a coordinated industrial network to meet India's strong long-term economic growth outlook.

news.title: Tulip Infratech to invest ₹1,100 crore in Gurugram housing project
news.para: Tulip Infratech Group is investing ₹1,100 crore in Tulip Melrose, a 7.5-acre residential project on Gurugram's Southern Peripheral Road. The development will feature four towers, aiming to be one of the tallest on the road at approximately 495 ft, with a focus on luxury residences and car-free ground level.

news.title: Himalia Prime Assets acquires eco-resort near Bengaluru for ₹25 crore
news.para: Himalia Prime Assets has acquired Our Native Village Eco Resort near Bengaluru for Rs 25 crore. The company plans to invest another Rs 25 crore over two years to upgrade the property. This acquisition brings Himalia's hospitality portfolio to three resorts. The resort will be managed by Niraamaya Life. Future expansion will increase inventory from 24 to 40 keys.

news.title: NCR states decide not to touch Aravali, conservation zone as Plan 2041 on hold
news.para: In a commendable move toward environmental sustainability, NCR states have committed to upholding the Natural Conservation Zone (NCZ) regulations, particularly for the Aravali range, as part of the Regional Plan 2041. To accommodate fresh inputs, the NCR Planning Board has opted to delay final decisions for two months, establishing a sub-committee tasked with delivering a comprehensive report by August 15.

news.title: Gurugram: Demolition drive against illegal commercial buildings begins on Galleria Road
news.para: Gurugram's enforcement wing demolished 13 unauthorized commercial structures in Chakkarpur, ending a two-decade legal battle. The action followed a Supreme Court directive to restore planned land use, with officials vowing to continue enforcement against illegal constructions across Gurgaon.

news.title: Harmony Infra Ventures to invest ₹550 crore in Ghaziabad housing project
news.para: Harmony Infra Ventures has acquired full ownership of The Horizon Residences, a ₹1,600 crore project in Indirapuram, Ghaziabad. The company plans to invest ₹550 crore in construction, with substructure work already in progress for the two G+33 towers. The project offers 264 residences, with prices starting from ₹5.99 crore.
